title: Publish @systembook/connector (and its workspace deps) to npm with a working CLI bin
description: Make the component-preview toolchain installable by external teams from npm. Today `@systembook/connector` is `private: true`, version `0.0.0`, has no `bin` field, and depends on two other unpublished workspace packages (`@systembook/schema`, `@systembook/preview-kit`) via `workspace:*` — so the setup doc's `pnpm add -D @systembook/connector` / `npx systembook-connector build` steps (TASK-60) cannot actually work yet. Publish the three packages so a design system repo can install and run the connector without cloning this monorepo.
acceptanceCriteria: `@systembook/schema`, `@systembook/preview-kit`, and `@systembook/connector` are published to npm (public access) with a real semver version, `private` removed, and `license` already set (MIT, from TASK-63)

`@systembook/connector` exposes a `bin` named `systembook-connector` that runs the CLI (`discover`/`generate`/`build`), so `npx systembook-connector build --root .` works from an installed package — not only `pnpm --filter ... cli`

The published packages resolve their inter-dependencies via real semver ranges (not `workspace:*`) — the publish tooling must rewrite `workspace:*` to the published versions (e.g. pnpm publish's workspace-protocol replacement), and `@systembook/schema` / `@systembook/preview-kit` are declared as runtime `dependencies` of the connector, not devDependencies

The published connector actually works: in a scratch repo OUTSIDE this monorepo, `pnpm add -D @systembook/connector`, add a `*.preview.tsx`, and `npx systembook-connector build --root .` produces the `.systembook/dist/` artifact + `manifest.json` (the TASK-60 example, verified end-to-end against the published tarball, e.g. via `npm pack` before a real publish)

Docs are updated to drop the pre-release caveats now that the package is real: the 'Pré-lançamento' note in `docs/setup.md` and the 'enquanto o pacote não está publicado' note in `docs/ci-example.md` are removed or adjusted to reference the published package

steps.description: Decide versioning and confirm npm org access
steps.details: Confirm the `@systembook` npm scope/org exists and the publisher has access. Pick an initial published version (recommend `0.1.0` to match the Docker image release line from TASK-58) and whether to keep the three packages version-locked together.

In `packages/schema`, `packages/preview-kit`, and `packages/connector`: remove `private: true`, set the chosen version, ensure `files`/`exports`/`types` are correct for consumers (schema and connector currently point `exports` at TS source under `src/` — decide whether to ship source-only or add a build; the connector's CLI must be runnable via the `bin`). Confirm `license: "MIT"` is present (from TASK-63).

steps.description: Add the CLI bin and validate it
steps.details: Add a `bin` entry `{ "systembook-connector": "<entry>" }` to the connector pointing at the CLI (`src/cli.ts` today runs via tsx; a published bin likely needs a Node-runnable entry — either a shebang'd built file or a bin shim, resolving the tsx-only dev setup). Verify `systembook-connector --help` and `build --root` run from an installed context.

Ensure `@systembook/schema` and `@systembook/preview-kit` are `dependencies` (runtime) of the connector, and that publishing rewrites `workspace:*` to the published semver (pnpm does this on `pnpm publish`). Publish the dependency graph in order (schema → preview-kit → connector).

steps.description: Verify end-to-end from outside the monorepo, then update docs
steps.details: Using `npm pack` tarballs (or a dry-run registry), install the connector into a scratch repo outside this monorepo, run the TASK-60 `button.preview.tsx` example through `npx systembook-connector build --root .`, and confirm the artifact + manifest are produced. Then remove the pre-release caveats in `docs/setup.md` and `docs/ci-example.md`.

technicalNotes: Surfaced during TASK-60: the setup doc documents `pnpm add -D @systembook/connector` / `npx systembook-connector build` but the package is unpublishable as-is (private, 0.0.0, no bin, workspace:* deps). This task closes that gap.

Key decision: ship TS source or add a build step. `@systembook/schema` is intentionally types-only (source exports); `@systembook/preview-kit` is bundled by the connector into the artifact; the connector itself needs a Node-executable bin. The current `exports.default` pointing at `src/*.ts` works for bundlers/tsx but NOT for `node` invoking the bin directly — resolve this deliberately.
Publish order matters because of the workspace dependency chain: schema → preview-kit → connector.
